item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://imgflip.com/i/2wbxjo

+1 for being both funny and SCP-esque. Outstanding writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this.
I admire your skills.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
so we took our only enemies: those damned rats. Those heretics deserve what's coming to them.

Since the current wording appears to be specifically referring to rats, linking to Giraffe Hell might be slightly disjointed. Unless there are rats in Giraffe Hell?

Perhaps it can be reworded to make 'heretic' more general instead of focusing on the rats as the heretics. Something like:

so we took our only enemies: those damned rats. Fur or fur-less, all heretics deserve what's coming to them.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a few potential crosslinks that could be worked in here. Continuing off the animal religion theme, its not too far of a stretch to have a potential link to Giraffe hell, as there may very well be its equivalent awaiting the foes of the order here. Additionally, there is a mention of the GOC within the article, which can be linked to that GoIs hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Following a joint siege by the Foundation and elements of the GOC, SCP-2050 was convinced to surrender and enter Foundation containment, in return for a supply of food and metal. Requests by SCP-2050 to be contained with SCP-1845 were denied.

To this:

Following a joint siege by the Foundation and elements of the GOC, SCP-2050 was convinced to surrender and enter Foundation containment, in return for a supply of food and metal. Requests by SCP-2050 to be contained with SCP-1845 were denied.

Changing this:

SCP-2050-1-58: 'Course. You fur-less lot seem like you know what you're going on about. We didn't know about any fur-less heretics though, so we took our only enemies: those damned rats. Those heretics deserve what's coming to them.

To this:

SCP-2050-1-58: 'Course. You fur-less lot seem like you know what you're going on about. We didn't know about any fur-less heretics though, so we took our only enemies: those damned rats. Those heretics deserve what's coming to them.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP was O.K., but I was forced to upvote mainly by my fond memories of reading The Redwall Series (think: Medieval mice, squirrels, and rodents with vaguely monk-like themes) in Middle School. Reading this pulled a heart-string.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For future reference: uncollapsed images aren't allowed in the forums. Please put that under a collapsible by doing this:

 [[collapsible]] (Image code) [[/collapsible]]

It should look like this:
+ show block
– hide block

(Image code)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll just leave this here…

+ show block
– hide block
()

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boy, this is golden. I'd double vote it, due to its relation to SCP-1845.
But, no can do voting twice. +1 will do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sapient Christian animals. Because this always works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is written well enough for me to not downvote, but the fact that it's more talking animals, with nothing that I haven't seen in all the other talking animal skips, particularly 1845, prevents me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how the SCP is so very well fleshed out. I have no fondness for Monty Pythonesque SCPs, though.

To avoid confusion, by Monty Pythonesque I mean serious traits applied to timid things (producing a cute result), like the schoolgirl spiders, this, and several other SCPs.

It is not that it is badly written, it is only not my cup of tea, theme-wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like 1845, and I think this is just basically the same concept done less well. Downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In which case, I'd like to see the interaction between these guys and 2050 fleshed out: in the form of some explicit cross-link in the article and/or a supplementary Tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wholeheartedly agree.

(Also, it'd be a smart move for containment purpose - Heaven forbids if the squirrels start getting a bit more self-reflective after we fur-less ones begin asking difficult questions, right?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only complaint here is that the interviewer should've said "I believe so" in regard to the "acorns in heaven" question. Why? Because it seems more human. You have an adorable furry little creature here that can dress in plate armour and declare war on heretics. Give him something. I know the Foundation tends hate Happiness with a passion that can only described as philic, but give the poor thing a bone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why can't I +1 a comment??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the author of 1845, I think this is distinct enough from Eugenio and his kingdom to stand as a mainlister. For that matter, Eugenio would probably consider these guys to be the heathens - they don't practice any of the sacraments in a meaningful manner, they're certainly not in communion with Rome, and I positively shudder to imagine where they stand on transubstantiation vs. consubstantiation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We are seriously oversaturated with talking animals at this point, and this one doesn't bring anything new to the table. It's not badly written but the concept has been absolutely done to death.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: Its all good, my man. Looking at yours, my idea would been DOA. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I'd paid more attention in chat; I thought you were writing an 1845 tale, not a whole article. If I'd realized that, I would have advised against it. As it stands, this is too similar to 1845 without either being directly related to it (in which case, I'm pretty sure they'd be part of that kingdom instead of a separate containment) or adding anything to the idea. And I still don't like 1845 because Redwall, so I'm not feeling this one either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rewrote the containment procedures to flow more logically, thanks to Kalinin's helpful critique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has nothing to do with cheesy British mannerisms that were thrown around in chat, because that never happened. I realize that this may sound like an excuse, but they are actually supposed to sound stupid. I do do research before writing these articles, and I try to keep as historically accurate as possible. Believe me when I say that this not supposed to be historically accurate dialogue. If it actually was, they'd be speaking Middle English. I kept them sounding stupid for the purposes of the article/story.

As for the containment procedures, I think that you have a very good point, and I'll see what I can do in terms of making it flow in a more logical way.

EDIT: Updated the containment procedures. Hope that these new ones are better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but rather squirrels attempting to sound like 12th century Britons. So if the dialogue is a little stupid, that's to be expected.

Yeah, no. I've heard the "but it's supposed to sound stupid" thing too many times. So now they're contemporary squirrels that happen to sound just like someone in a chat room doing a cheesy British mannerisms imitation who happen to think they're crusader squirrels? Sorry. No sale.

The containment procedures are there because many people pointed out the obviousness of a citadel, even in a rather remote and large forested area.

Yeah. Which is why it would be far easier to tear it down and relocate the anomaly than inventing what amounts to an invisibility field.

I'd also argue that keeping them there is an easier expense than attempting to contain over 200, angry and militaristic squirrels.

They're squirrels. Squirrels with no knowledge of advanced tactics, access to modern weaponry, or even the ability to complete tasks at that. Cash-strapped zoos manage to contain far more dangerous animals on a regular basis.

If the Foundation rounded them up, now you have to provide for food, containment space, psychological counseling for squirrels and whatnot that would ultimately make it more expensive than letting them go free.

Food for a couple of hundred squirrels, space in an existing facility, and some researchers, who are working on the project anyway, is by any stretch of the imagination far cheaper than inventing and deploying new technology, contingency planning for when someone inevitably spots one of these things and has to be detained and amnesticized, clandestine embedding of multiple security personnel on an indefinite basis, and scrubbing all known satellite imagery for the site. There is no way that explanation holds up to any sort of rational examination. The containment detracts from the article for me, and I suspect for anyone else that considers these kind of things when reading articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried to not make them sound like 12th century Britons, but rather squirrels attempting to sound like 12th century Britons. So if the dialogue is a little stupid, that's to be expected. They're not smart. They're trying to imitate what they think the Britons sounded like. If I wanted to make them historically accurate, I'd have pulled out a book on Occitan or consulted my Chaucer knowledge.

The containment procedures are there because many people pointed out the obviousness of a citadel, even in a rather remote and large forested area. I'd also argue that keeping them there is an easier expense than attempting to contain over 200, angry and militaristic squirrels. Right now, they're self-sustaining. The Foundation doesn't need to actively provide for these things, and coercing it into staying isolationist is more than effective at containing them. If the Foundation rounded them up, now you have to provide for food, containment space, psychological counseling for squirrels and whatnot that would ultimately make it more expensive than letting them go free.

I appreciate the critique though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ line-by-line
- close

Optical illusions and distortion are to be employed in order to disguise the existence of the citadel of SCP-2050. Satellite imagery is to be monitored in order to remove the presence of the citadel housing SCP-2050.

The containment procedures are beyond ridiculous. I feel like what I'm seeing here is an overreaction to the excessive cruelty to sapient skips that marked earlier articles, and now we're embedding personnel, monitoring and scrubbing satellite data, establishing diplomatic relations, and hiding a miniature city behind Harry Potter invisibility cloaks and Magic Eye paintings or whatever the hell so that we don't have to imprison something. Why? That's excessive use of resources when you can just gather the little bastards up and stick them in a pen somewhere.

Metal for this armor is currently supplied by the Foundation, in order to maintain good relations with SCP-2050.

Why would we do this? There is no reason the Foundation can't establish containment here. Who cares if we have good relations or not?

However, SCP-2050-1 instances are unable to focus on complex tasks for long periods of time, and often lose interest in tasks before completion.

How the hell did they build a stone citadel, weapons, and armor then?

containment of SCP-2050 is to focus on preventing outside awareness of SCP-2050.

I feel compelled to say once again that there are far easier ways of doing this than what you outlined above.

Why, yes, I do believe that you are right, good sir.

Amen, brother. Say, is that an acorn over yonder?

Take that back, you vagabond! I dare say, take that back this instant!

No more of these lowborn insults! Taste my wrath, Bushtail fiend!

I just know that if I kill enough of those heretics, I get rewarded, and go to heaven or something.

This dialogue. It burns. The implication is that since this is on the mainlist, I'm supposed to take this seriously. If for some reason I could take "talking squirrel knights" seriously, this would completely destroy that.

As has been stated upthread, this is very close thematically to King Eugenio, the main differences being that it's not as well realized, it's squirrels-only, and we let them run around free because gosh darn it it's just so funny. The addenda are too short and pretty much only reinforce what we already know, making them essentially article filler. And if you're going to make them talk like 12th century Britons, at least try to not make them sound like a 21st century American's passing idea of what an English guy sounds like based on Monty Python sketches and episodes of Downton Abbey.

I get the impression that this is riding the "look it's from chat lolsorandom" thing, coasting on novelty rather than any sort of innate narrative quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't even.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I.. I can't I just can't. All I can think about is this. They are just tiny, furry knights and the squirrel jokes and.. no. Stop being so silly. This is a joke.

Edit: With the revisions I have come to accept the squirrel crusading brethren. I can now dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would've liked it more if it included actual squirrel behavior as interpreted by psuedo crusader mentality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I normally dislike cross-linking between articles, but in this case I think it would help a lot if there were official links between this and 1845.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I should like crusaders squirrels, but for some reason those don't manage to interest me. Maybe because they are not developed enough for my taste, or that the idea of speaking squirrels is a bit too silly for a non-J SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

Yes, it's similar to 1845, but that just means that there are connections between the two, which means more tale fodder for me and worldbuilding on the whole (as in, Catholic talking animals are an established Thing in the Foundationverse)

Yes, it's similar to Redwall, but 1) I know that this was not intentional 2) They act nothing like the squirrels from the aforementioned series 3) I fail to see any context or situation where that is a bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that I've actually read the finished article, I have to say it certainly does remind me of Redwall quite a bit. I know you didn't intend to copy that, and it's certainly not a perfect copy, but the similarities are distracting enough that I'm going to stay neutral on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reads more like a -J than an actual article. The ridiculousness of holy squirrel crusaders combined with the sterile tone of the report is admittedly pretty entertaining, but I noticed this has a lot of similarities to this article which explored the concept in a much more engaging way.

The dialogue between the squirrels is also really over the top and cheesy. Even if that's what you were going for, "Why, yes, I do believe that you are right, good sir" was pretty hard to stomach. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like SCP-1845, except not as fleshed out or amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cain is in the mood for silliness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at "Sciurine Monastic Brotherhood of Poor-Fellows and Crusader Knights,"

However, SCP-2050-1 instances are unable to focus on complex tasks for long periods of time, and often lose interest in tasks before completion.

I said you had me at "Sciurine Monastic Brotherhood of Poor-Fellows and Crusader Knights,", why do you keep drawing me in?

Say, is that an acorn over yonder?

STAHP

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
This was written from sudden inspiration in site 19 when someone linked a photo of a squirrel in armor. My insane brain thought "Squirrel crusaders," and this popped out. Sorry. Also, we need more Sciurine SCP's.

Special thanks to Bucell for being a good sport about the fact that we were both writing a draft about squirrel knights.

Other thanks go to all of the folks in chat who took a look, and entertained this insane notion.
